role: This file is the global, subscription-based data store for the channel graph view, fetching and caching /api/channels rows for React consumers.
capabilities: Fetch /api/channels and normalize rows into ChannelRow shape
Hold channels, loading, error, and open/closed UI state
Provide a React useChannels hook via manual subscription set
Expose openChannels, closeChannels, and loadChannels actions
Guard against stale fetch races with a loadSeq counter
substance_why: It implements a complete fetch-normalize-cache-subscribe cycle with state management and React integration, despite an empty startup stub.
interactions_present: openChannels action refreshes and reveals the channel view
closeChannels action hides the channel view
useChannels hook subscribes React components to store updates
loadChannels fetches and normalizes channel rows
interactions_missing: No auto-load on mount (ensureStarted is an empty stub)
No retry UI or action despite the fail-loud retry claim
No refresh, pagination, search, filter, or sort
No channel selection or drill-down interaction
Members object-to-array normalization is deferred to the call site
performance_risks: Every state update notifies all subscribers and returns a new state object, causing broad re-renders
No memoization or selector support in useChannels
No request deduplication or caching TTL; openChannels triggers a fresh fetch each time
Large channel arrays are re-mapped and stored in a single global object on every load
reuse_why: Keep the API normalization, row shape, and race-guard logic, but replace the hand-rolled mutable subscription store with a typed, modern state solution.
decomposition_hints: Split API fetching and normalization into a dedicated channels API client/adapter
Split the custom subscription mechanism into a reusable store hook or replace with an off-the-shelf store
Separate channel data cache from open/close UI visibility state
Move members-object normalization into the adapter instead of the call site
